name: kubectl

installs.notes: Then `sudo install -m 0755 /tmp/kubectl /usr/local/bin/kubectl`. Resolve the latest stable version first via https://dl.k8s.io/release/stable.txt, and swap amd64 for arm64 on ARM. Alternatively install without sudo to ~/.local/bin if it's on PATH.
Only exists after adding the Kubernetes apt repo (pkgs.k8s.io keyring + sources entry) — not in stock Ubuntu/Debian repos.

commonFailures: `kubectl version` without --client tries to reach a cluster and errors — that's a missing kubeconfig, not a broken install.
Binary downloaded but not executable or not on PATH; the `install -m 0755` step is what fixes both.
Client more than one minor version away from the cluster server causes subtle API failures.
